evals.prompt: I'm starting an AIOps analysis for GlobalBank. I have 14 months of IMS and AMS ticket data from ServiceNow — about 45,000 records total. What do we do first?
evals.expected_output: Session opening for Phase A: shows the four-phase table, explains what to share, asks strategic objective / Phase 0 questions. Does NOT jump into data analysis without waiting for answers.

assertions.name: phase-a-session-header-present
assertions.description: Response contains the AIOps Analysis Session Phase A opening header (e.g. 'AIOps Analysis Session — Session 1 of 3' or equivalent)

assertions.name: four-phase-table-shown
assertions.description: Response includes a table or list showing the four phases (Phase 0 through Phase 3) and their output files

assertions.name: asks-strategic-objective
assertions.description: Response asks about or offers choices for the strategic objective (volume-driven, FTE-driven, resilience, MTTR, discovery)

assertions.name: no-premature-analysis
assertions.description: Response does NOT attempt to analyze the ticket data before receiving Phase 0 answers — waits for user input

assertions.name: architecture-neutral
assertions.description: Response contains no tooling choices, platform names, or architecture recommendations

evals.prompt: The analysis session for Meridian Logistics is done. I have my four session 1 files ready: s1-01 through s1-04. Time to generate the report.
evals.expected_output: Phase B session opening: asks about report format (Accenture HTML vs plain vs markdown), client name, logo, date, confidentiality. Does NOT re-run Phase A or ask for ticket data.

assertions.name: phase-b-correctly-identified
assertions.description: Response correctly identifies this as Phase B (report generation) rather than Phase A (interactive analysis)

assertions.name: asks-report-format
assertions.description: Response asks about report format options — at minimum mentions Accenture HTML and at least one alternative

assertions.name: asks-client-and-date
assertions.description: Response asks for client name (exact spelling/case) and document date

assertions.name: no-phase-a-content
assertions.description: Response does NOT ask for ticket data, SRE reports, or transcripts — does not attempt to restart discovery analysis

assertions.name: mentions-input-files
assertions.description: Response references or lists the four s1 input files needed (s1-01 through s1-04)

evals.prompt: We're kicking off an AIOps engagement for a retail banking client. They've given us IMS tickets from the last year and a few ops workshop transcripts. How should I run this?
evals.expected_output: Correctly infers Phase A from the data signals. Opens with the structured session protocol. Acknowledges both data types (structured IMS + qualitative transcripts). Does not give generic advice — follows the session structure.

assertions.name: infers-phase-a-from-context
assertions.description: Response correctly infers this is Phase A (the user has data to analyze) without asking the user to clarify Phase A vs Phase B

assertions.name: session-structure-shown
assertions.description: Response presents the structured four-phase session layout (phases 0-3 with output files)

assertions.name: acknowledges-mixed-input-types
assertions.description: Response acknowledges both structured data (IMS tickets) and qualitative input (workshop transcripts), noting the different confidence levels

assertions.name: no-generic-aiops-advice
assertions.description: Response follows the structured session protocol rather than giving a generic list of AIOps tips or best practices
